text.title: ASP.NET Core Code Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: Controller, service, IExceptionHandler, and integration test examples; plus antipatterns to reject.

example.note: CancellationToken on every action. No DbContext, no business logic. Returns DTO (never entity).
TimeProvider injected for testable time. CancellationToken on SaveChangesAsync. Returns DTO, not entity.

antipattern.description: DbContext injected into a singleton service (captive dependency).
antipattern.reason: DbContext is scoped; singleton holds it for the process lifetime, causing state corruption and connection leaks under concurrent requests.

antipattern.description: Returning EF tracked entity from controller or service public method.
antipattern.reason: Circular JSON serialization, internal schema exposure, and lazy-load exceptions outside the DbContext scope.

antipattern.description: UseAuthentication and UseAuthorization in wrong order in Program.cs.
antipattern.reason: UseAuthorization reads the ClaimsPrincipal that UseAuthentication sets. Wrong order means [Authorize] checks an anonymous principal and silently allows all requests.
